column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-pointer-gestures (pg-01 … pg-11).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Absence defects
# (pg-02/03/05 missing affordance) are located by surfacing interactive-view CANDIDATES; the agent judges
# absence after READ. Structural rules grep cannot express (gate-scope, gesture co-occurrence) live in

column4: INVENTED pointer case → PointerStyle.grabActive / .grabIdle (macOS 15). Stale .grabbing does not exist. fix_mode: auto
pointer modifier present — REPORT pg-07 only if deployment target < its floor (pointerStyle 15, onContinuousHover 14; read in ORIENT); else ignore. VERIFY gated #available(macOS NN, *).
DEPRECATED (26.5) → MagnifyGesture (macOS 14). Owns the rewrite mechanics; cross_ref api-currency for the flag (pg-08).
DEPRECATED (26.5) → RotateGesture (macOS 14). Owns the rewrite mechanics; cross_ref api-currency for the flag (pg-09).
custom interactive view CANDIDATE — READ it: if it reacts to clicks/drag but has NO .onHover, it is dead under the pointer (pg-02). A static label is fine.
resize/drag handle CANDIDATE — READ it: a draggable/resizable affordance with NO .pointerStyle gives no cursor shape (pg-03, macOS 15).
binary .onHover — if the site needs the live pointer CGPoint (crosshair, value-under-cursor, tracking tooltip), use onContinuousHover (macOS 14) instead (pg-04, UNVERIFIED judgment).
row/item container CANDIDATE — READ it: if a row has action Buttons but NO right-click .contextMenu, the Mac idiom is missing (pg-05).
swipe-to-act is the touch idiom — if it is the ONLY way to act on the row, add a right-click .contextMenu on macOS (pg-06).
continuous gesture — READ it: if it has no @GestureState (nor a committed @State written in .onChanged/.updating), the in-flight value is lost (pg-10).

plain .gesture — if attached to a control that already has a built-in gesture (Button/Slider/ScrollView), it may swallow it → .simultaneousGesture / .highPriorityGesture (pg-11, verify against Xcode 26 SDK).
